beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Across eleven AI-spreadsheet combinations, only Claude Cowork produced a finished, polished Excel you could hand to a board. Setup: the desktop app from claude.com/download, a Pro account, the Cowork tab, a folder, plus Opus 4.7 and Adaptive thinking. The reusable template names your starting data, who uses the sheet and why, the sheets and rows needed, and the formatting rules. The magic closing line — list your top ten assumptions before you build — puts you in control and prevents a wrong spreadsheet.

artifactLines: Across eleven AI-spreadsheet combinations, only Claude Cowork produced a finished, polished Excel you could hand to a board.
Setup: the desktop app from claude.com/download, a Pro account, the Cowork tab, a folder, plus Opus 4.7 and Adaptive thinking.
The reusable template names your starting data, who uses the sheet and why, the sheets and rows needed, and the formatting rules.
The magic closing line — list your top ten assumptions before you build — puts you in control and prevents a wrong spreadsheet.

beats.narration_text: The 'list your top ten assumptions before you build' line is the part I want to master — it's the handshake that keeps the spreadsheet from being wrong. For my own recurring task, a twelve-month revenue forecast with four service lines: (1) show me how to fill the template's four context slots for that specific job, (2) give me an example of the ten good assumptions Cowork should surface before touching a cell, and (3) tell me how to push back on an assumption I disagree with so the rebuild stays board-ready.

props.command: The 'list your top ten assumptions before you build' line is the part I want to master — it's the handshake that keeps the spreadsheet from being wrong. For my own recurring task, a twelve-month revenue forecast with four service lines: (1) show me how to fill the template's four context slots for that specific job, (2) give me an example of the ten good assumptions Cowork should surface before touching a cell, and (3) tell me how to push back on an assumption I disagree with so the rebuild stays board-ready.

beats.narration_text: Test eleven different AI-plus-spreadsheet combinations. Copilot failed completely. Grok said 'done' but couldn't produce a file you could actually open. ChatGPT inside Google Sheets is decent for edits. But Claude Cowork — the one that runs many Claudes at once — is the only tool that produces a finished, polished Excel you can open and hand to a board.

artifactLines: Copilot → couldn't make a spreadsheet at all.
Grok → says done, no file appears.
ChatGPT inside Google Sheets → good for edits, not creation.
Claude Cowork → the only one that builds a finished, polished Excel.

beats.narration_text: Quick access reminder before we dive into the prompt. You need the Claude desktop app — not the browser. Go to claude.com/download. You need at least a Pro account at twenty dollars a month. Open the app. Click the Cowork tab at the top — it sits between Chat and Code. Select a folder from your computer. Then make sure you've got Opus 4.7 and Adaptive thinking turned on. That's the whole setup.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B02.png (screenshot: Claude desktop app, Cowork tab selected, model picker showing Opus 4.7 + Adaptive thinking on)

beats.narration_text: Now the prompt. This reusable template works for recurring consulting and reporting tasks. The structure: what data you're starting from, who uses the spreadsheet and why, what sheets you need and what each row represents, and the formatting rules. The magic line at the end is the one that actually saves you: list your top ten assumptions before you build. That puts you in control, not the AI.

props.title: cowork-spreadsheet-prompt.md
props.code:
Create an Excel spreadsheet from:
[DATA: file path, folder, or pasted data]. ### Purpose:
[Who uses this and what decision it supports — 1 sentence.] ### Sheets needed:
- "[Sheet name]": [columns, what each row is, formulas]
- "[Sheet name]": [summary, pivot, charts] ### Formatting:
[Currency/dates, conditional highlighting, frozen headers, totals] Before building, list your top 10 assumptions
so I can sanity-check them, then execute.

beats.narration_text: Here's what that prompt looks like in the real world. a consulting firm needs a twelve-month revenue forecast — board-ready, four service lines with very different margins, enough detail that the board can pressure-test every assumption live in the meeting. Paste the template, fill in the context, and Cowork returns with ten assumptions to approve before touching a single cell. That's the handshake that prevents the spreadsheet from being wrong.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B04.png (screenshot: Cowork session showing the ten-assumptions list Claude generated before building the revenue forecast)

beats.narration_text: Once you approve the assumptions, Cowork builds. It creates the actual Excel file and drops it in your folder — not a preview, not a screenshot, the real file you open in Excel or Google Sheets. Multiple tabs, real formulas, conditional formatting. The same process can produce recurring board reports every week. The whole thing takes under five minutes from prompt to file.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B05.png (screenshot: the finished Excel file open in Excel or Google Sheets, showing multiple tabs with real formulas and formatting)

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude Cowork — Opus 4.7, Adaptive thinking on, your folder selected. Fill in the brackets. Let Claude list assumptions before it builds. Then approve and get your file.

props.command: Create an Excel spreadsheet from: [my data]. Purpose: [who uses it, what decision]. Sheets: [Sheet 1: columns, rows]. Formatting: [currency, frozen header]. Before building, list your top 10 assumptions so I can sanity-check them, then execute.

props.title: Stop learning Excel.
props.subline: Cowork builds the file for you
